metric: Attended fires with solar/PV terms in incident free text
sourceTitle: MHCLG Incident Recording System, Table A37
caveat: A PV term may describe presence rather than ignition. Free-text search is neither complete nor proof of causation.

metric: Fire-service incidents involving a solar panel
sourceTitle: QBE UK fire-service FOI research
caveat: Overlaps England IRS data and must not be added to it. QBE reports 107 incidents in 2022, 128 in 2023 and 171 in 2024.

metric: Building fires where a solar-energy system was present
sourceTitle: TNO / NIPV / NEN building-fire study
caveat: Of 152 fires, 70 were investigated further: 30 originated in the solar system, 29 did not and 11 remained unknown.

metric: Fire incidents associated with solar panels
sourceClass: Regulator consultation citing Fire and Rescue NSW
sourceTitle: Energy Safe Victoria DC-isolator consultation
caveat: Annual figures were 22 in 2018, 56 in 2019 and 139 in 2020. Association does not necessarily mean PV ignition.

metric: Fires involving solar installations
sourceClass: Regulator consultation citing Country Fire Authority
sourceTitle: Energy Safe Victoria DC-isolator consultation
caveat: The period starts before the dashboard window. CFA believed DC isolators caused 65 of the 188 incidents.

metric: Fires where the PV system was considered the cause
sourceClass: Regulator consultation citing Fire Rescue Victoria/MFB
sourceTitle: Energy Safe Victoria DC-isolator consultation
caveat: The period starts before the dashboard window; 41 incidents (85%) were believed caused by DC isolators.

metric: Unique potential PV-related incidents investigated
sourceTitle: BRE Fire and Solar PV Systems—Investigations and Evidence
caveat: Includes 33 historical incidents. Of 80 records, 16 were not caused by PV and six were unknown; 22 PV-caused events were serious and 36 localized or thermal.

metric: Fires involving PV systems
sourceTitle: Fraunhofer ISE fire-protection research
caveat: Outside the dashboard's ten-year window; retained only as a benchmark and never included in current-period totals.

metric: Residential PV systems reported smoking or catching fire
sourceTitle: Japan Consumer Safety Investigation Commission findings
caveat: Most of the reporting period predates the dashboard window. Thirteen examined cases involved fire from modules or cables; seven spread to the roof.

metric: Walmart store fires attributed to rooftop solar in the retailer's complaint
sourceTitle: Walmart Inc. v. Tesla Inc. complaint
caveat: These are allegations from settled litigation, not seven independent official cause determinations. One 2012 incident falls outside the current window; the six newer locations are also represented as reported case records and must not be double-counted.

metric: Critical fire or arc-flash events in Amazon's internal solar-facility review
sourceTitle: CNBC investigation of Amazon rooftop-solar shutdown
caveat: CNBC reported six events among 47 applicable North American sites (12.7%). The other locations were not publicly disclosed, so this portfolio count is not converted into six map pins and may overlap the Perryville case.

metric: No defensible national PV-fire total from the public NFIRS coding fields
sourceTitle: U.S. Fire Administration NFIRS Complete Reference Guide
caveat: NFIRS code 115 records solar panels only as an optional factor affecting suppression, not as equipment or cause of ignition. Generic equipment codes for wiring, panels, disconnects and inverters do not identify a PV system, so raw NFIRS cannot cleanly count PV-origin fires.

metric: National rooftop-PV fire count tracked by the U.S. Fire Administration
sourceTitle: U.S. Department of Energy guide to fire safety in solar systems
caveat: DOE states that the U.S. Fire Administration does not track the number of fires caused by rooftop PV systems. It also distinguishes PV-origin fires from ordinary building fires that later involve a PV-covered roof.

metric: PV-panel fire incidents in the national fire brigade statistical database; roughly 5–13 fires per GW installed annually
sourceTitle: CNVVF statistical database, analysed in Rus et al. 2025 (J. Phys.: Conf. Ser. 3121 012050)
caveat: A dedicated “Photovoltaic Panels” category exists since 2014. Counts reflect incidents where PV modules were involved in the fire, not proof that PV caused it. Earlier surveys recorded roughly 2,500 PV-related incidents across ~550,000 systems for 2002–2015.

metric: PV-related fire deployments reported to the 112 emergency system; roughly 37 fires per GW installed annually
sourceTitle: URSZR incident reporting, analysed in Rus et al. 2025 (J. Phys.: Conf. Ser. 3121 012050)
caveat: A PV-related category was only added to the reporting form in mid-2023, so the observation window is short and annual figures are extrapolated. Inclusion criteria may capture fires where a PV system was present but not the ignition source.

metric: Weighted international average of ~29 PV-related fires per GW of installed capacity per year
sourceTitle: Four-country comparative study, reaffirmed in Rus et al. 2025 (J. Phys.: Conf. Ser. 3121 012050)
caveat: National methodologies differ substantially and had to be harmonised; the benchmark expresses reporting frequency relative to installed capacity, not comparative safety of products or installers.

metric: Emergency-response incidents involving PV systems recorded by Beredskabsstyrelsen
sourceTitle: Beredskabsstyrelsen: Flere solcelleanlæg betyder ikke nødvendigvis flere brande
caveat: Incident involvement of a PV system does not establish the PV system as the ignition source; the agency notes fire counts have not grown in step with installations.

metric: Fires and fire incidents linked to solar-cell installations in rescue-service incident reports
sourceTitle: Statistik om bränder i solcellsanläggningar (Swedish rescue-service data compilation)
caveat: Compiled from MSB incident-report free text; classification of PV as cause versus involvement varies across municipal rescue services.

metric: Fires in buildings or facilities with PV installations recorded by the State Fire Service (KG PSP): 808 in 2024, 759 in 2025 (to 10 Nov), 109 in Q1 2026
sourceTitle: KG PSP data reported by Gram w Zielone: Pożary fotowoltaiki
caveat: Counts are fires in objects where a PV installation was present, not necessarily fires ignited by the PV system.

metric: Fires involving PV power plants recorded by the Czech fire service (HZS ČR); 83 in 2024 alone, of which 59 caused by the PV system
sourceTitle: HZS ČR data reported by Novinky.cz: V Česku výrazně roste počet požárů fotovoltaických elektráren
caveat: confirmedPvOrigin figure applies to 2024 only; earlier-year cause attribution is not broken out in the report.

metric: Accidents involving solar power generation equipment reported to NITE over 10 years; about 90% (239) were fires, with power conditioners (170 cases) the leading component
sourceTitle: NITE mail magazine Vol.487: 太陽光発電設備の事故
caveat: NITE receives reports on consumer-product accidents; utility-scale plant fires outside the reporting scheme are not captured.

metric: PV-related fires recorded by the National Fire Agency: 62 (2019), 69 (2020), 81 (2021), 99 (2022), 124 (2023); ~7 in 10 attributed to electrical factors
sourceTitle: National Fire Agency data reported by Newsis: 최근 5년 태양광 화재 44억 피해
caveat: Aggregate includes fires at PV facilities of all scales; electrical-factor attribution does not distinguish module, inverter, or wiring origin.

metric: Solar-panel structure fires in Fire and Emergency NZ callout data released under the Official Information Act
sourceTitle: FENZ OIA response 19266: Callout data for solar-related fires
caveat: Callout coding relies on attending crews recording solar involvement; low counts partly reflect NZ's small installed base.

metric: Solar panel-related fires reported to Energy Safe Victoria: 27 (2021–22) rising to 55 (2022–23); DC-isolator fires rose from 15 to 41
sourceTitle: ABC News: Solar panel fires increase prompts DC isolator warning
caveat: Complements the CFA/MFB brigade series already tracked; regulator reporting captures notified electrical incidents rather than all brigade callouts.

metric: Documented fire cases involving PV systems out of roughly 145,000 installed systems in the province
sourceTitle: ORF Niederösterreich: PV-Anlagen – Montagemängel als Brandrisiko (Landesstelle für Brandverhütung)
caveat: The fire-prevention office highlights installation defects as the dominant cause; only investigated cases are counted.

metric: Incidents involving photovoltaic panels recorded in the national ARIA industrial-accident database
sourceTitle: Ministère de la Transition écologique, base ARIA: Accidentologie photovoltaïque
caveat: ARIA covers classified installations and notable accidents, so small residential fires are under-represented; period predates the 2016+ incident window.

metric: Solar-panel fires attended in 2024 across 37 of 49 fire services: 97 residential, 27 commercial, 17 solar farms (QBE FOI, submitted in planning evidence)
sourceClass: Insurer FOI data in planning evidence
sourceTitle: Planning Inspectorate submission EN010170: Fire Hazard and BESS installation proposals at Grendon
caveat: Extends the QBE 2022–2024 series already tracked with a property-type breakdown for 2024; 12 fire services did not respond to the FOI.

metric: Rooftop PV systems audited worldwide; 97% showed at least one major fire or electrical safety concern (49% grounding issues, 47% cross-mated connectors)
sourceTitle: Clean Energy Associates: Top 10 PV Rooftop Safety Risks
caveat: Audit sample skews to commercial and industrial rooftops whose owners commissioned inspections; findings are risk indicators, not fire counts.

metric: Fire identified as the second-largest loss driver at PV sites; 84% of PV fire events are equipment-driven brushfires originating within the plant
sourceTitle: kWh Analytics: 2026 Solar Risk Assessment
caveat: Based on insured-loss and claims data for utility-scale assets; percentages describe loss events, not a census of all fires.

metric: Photovoltaic Failure Fact Sheets cataloguing module and system failure modes, including those with fire consequences, from Task 13 field data
sourceTitle: IEA PVPS Task 13: Photovoltaic Failure Fact Sheets (PVFS) 2025
caveat: A failure-mode taxonomy rather than an incident census; fire-relevant failures are a subset of documented degradation modes.
